$description.$value: Component dimensions for the Small UI Scale mode — values derived from base primitives × sm multiplier. Dynamic tokens: scale automatically when the Small mode is active.

px.$description: Zero dimension for collapse and reset. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size.

rem.$description: Zero dimension for collapse and reset. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility.

px.$description: Finest hairline dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for 1px borders and sub-pixel rules.

rem.$description: Finest hairline dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable hairlines that adapt to user preferences.

px.$description: Thin border dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for subtle dividers, thin outlines, nudge adjustments.

rem.$description: Thin border dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable dividers and outlines that adapt to user preferences.

px.$description: Visible border dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for prominent outlines and focus indicators.

rem.$description: Visible border dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable outlines and focus indicators.

px.$description: Minimal component-internal dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for tight fixed-size padding and small icon gaps.

rem.$description: Minimal component-internal dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable padding, icon gaps, accessible element internals.

px.$description: Small component-internal dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for compact component gaps and fine internal adjustments.

rem.$description: Small component-internal dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable component gaps that adapt to user preferences.

px.$description: Standard component-internal dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for typical component padding and grid alignment.

rem.$description: Standard component-internal dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable component padding and accessible layout grids.

px.$description: Enhanced component-internal dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for comfortable internal spacing and improved legibility.

rem.$description: Enhanced component-internal dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for generous scalable spacing that enhances legibility.

px.$description: Large component-internal dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for spacious component padding and prominent internal gaps.

rem.$description: Large component-internal dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for spacious scalable padding in larger components.

px.$description: Small content spacing dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for fixed gaps between sibling elements and standard content separation.

rem.$description: Small content spacing dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable gaps between elements that respect user preferences.

px.$description: Medium content spacing dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for standard fixed section gaps.

rem.$description: Medium content spacing dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable section gaps.

px.$description: Large content spacing dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for clear fixed separation between content areas.

rem.$description: Large content spacing dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable separation between content areas.

px.$description: Enhanced content spacing dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for prominent fixed visual separation.

rem.$description: Enhanced content spacing dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for prominent scalable visual separation.

px.$description: Extra-large content spacing dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for significant fixed visual breaks between sections.

rem.$description: Extra-large content spacing dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for significant scalable breaks between sections.

px.$description: Small container padding dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for tight fixed container internals.

rem.$description: Small container padding dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable container padding.

px.$description: Medium container padding dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for standard fixed card and panel padding.

rem.$description: Medium container padding dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable card and panel padding.

px.$description: Large container padding dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for generous fixed container padding.

rem.$description: Large container padding dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for generous scalable container padding.

px.$description: Enhanced container padding dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for spacious fixed containers and prominent visual areas.

rem.$description: Enhanced container padding dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for spacious scalable containers.

px.$description: Extra-large container padding dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for maximum fixed container breathing room.

rem.$description: Extra-large container padding dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for maximum scalable container spacing.

px.$description: Small layout gap dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for fixed spacing between major page sections.

rem.$description: Small layout gap dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable spacing between page sections.

px.$description: Medium layout gap dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for clear fixed separation between independent page areas.

rem.$description: Medium layout gap dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable separation between page areas.

px.$description: Large layout gap dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for significant fixed gaps between major layout elements.

rem.$description: Large layout gap dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for significant scalable layout gaps.

px.$description: Enhanced layout gap dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for generous fixed page-level spacing.

rem.$description: Enhanced layout gap dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for generous scalable page-level spacing.

px.$description: Extra-large layout gap dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for maximum fixed page-level separation.

rem.$description: Extra-large layout gap dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for maximum scalable page-level separation.

px.$description: Small page-level dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for hero sections and feature-area spacing.

rem.$description: Small page-level dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for scalable hero sections and feature areas.

px.$description: Large page-level dimension. Absolute (px) — does not scale with browser font size. Use for maximum page-level spacing and full-width features.

rem.$description: Large page-level dimension. Fluid (rem) — scales with root font size for accessibility. Use for maximum scalable page-level features.
